intro: The most concrete split between RevenueCat and Adapty is the pricing model: RevenueCat runs on usage-based pricing from 1% from MTR above $2,500, with a free tier, while Adapty uses subscription from $0/mo, with a free tier. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, RevenueCat carries medium lock-in (medium — to on storekit/google play, but subscription state + analytics andin) and usage-based pricing from 1% from MTR above $2,500, with a free tier; Adapty carries medium lock-in (subscription sdk wraps app store/play; purchase data exportable) and subscription from $0/mo, with a free tier. RevenueCat is a fit when you are mobile. Adapty is a fit when you are saas. The honest trade-off: RevenueCat is explicitly not for web-only saas; Adapty is explicitly not for simple projects. On the product side, RevenueCat is described as Subscription infrastructure SDK for iOS, Android, and web handling purchase events, entitlements, and revenue analytics, and Adapty is described as In-app subscription SDK for iOS and Android with.
quick_take: RevenueCat is for mobile; Adapty is for saas; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose RevenueCat if its usage pricing model fits your budget shape better than Adapty's subscription model, and you are mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Adapty if its subscription pricing model fits your budget shape better than RevenueCat's usage model, and you are saas.

faq.a: RevenueCat starts at 1% from MTR above $2,500; Adapty starts at $0/mo. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from RevenueCat to Adapty?
faq.a: Migration from RevenueCat to Adapty is feasible but not trivial: RevenueCat has medium lock-in (medium — to on storekit/google play, but subscription state + analytics andin). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring RevenueCat rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Adapty. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in RevenueCat. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Adapty a good alternative to RevenueCat?
faq.a: Adapty is pitched at saas, which overlaps with RevenueCat's fit (mobile) but is not identical. Note that Adapty is explicitly not for simple projects, so it only replaces RevenueCat if you are outside that exclusion. Pricing models differ (usage vs subscription), which may shift total cost.
